ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee works in */,
  "title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the employee */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date the employee was hired */,
  "employee_status" TEXT /* Current status of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job title of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES employee_titles ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the department */,
  "department_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  "head_of_department" INTEGER /* Reference to the head of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_head_of_department FOREIGN KEY ("head_of_department") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wages" (
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wage record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the employee receiving the wage */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the wage was recorded */,
  "annual_wages" REAL /* Annual wages of the employee */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the wage data */,
  "wage_type" TEXT /* Type of wage (e.g., base salary, overtime, bonus) */,
  "pay_grade" TEXT /* Pay grade of the employee */,
  "step" INTEGER /* Step within the pay grade */,
  "gross_wages" REAL /* Gross wages before deductions */,
  "net_wages" REAL /* Net wages after deductions */,
  "deductions" REAL /* Total deductions from the gross wages */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wages_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wages_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES wage_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wage_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source of wage data */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "source_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary trend record */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the trend data */,
  "average_salary" REAL /* Average salary for the year across all departments */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department related to the trend */,
  "average_gross_salary" REAL /* Average gross salary for the year */,
  "average_net_salary" REAL /* Average net salary for the year */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the year (e.g., minimum, maximum) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_trends_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active or not */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the wage data */,
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* ID of the wage record being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_action" TEXT /* Action performed during the access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "access_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the access (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_wage_id FOREIGN KEY ("wage_id") REFERENCES wages ("wage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_titles" (
  "title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "title_name" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job title */,
  "title_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the job title */,
  "title_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary analysis record */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department related to the analysis */,
  "average_wage" REAL /* Average wage calculated for the department */,
  "role_summary" TEXT /* Summary of roles present in the department */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date the analysis was performed */,
  "average_salary_range" TEXT /* Average salary range for the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_analysis_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "department_hierarchies" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hierarchy record */,
  "parent_department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent department */,
  "child_department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the child department */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the department in the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_department_hierarchies_parent_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_department_hierarchies_child_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("child_department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id")
)
